title: Here’s Why Men’s World Cup Athletes Wear Sports Bras
author: Bob Grant
text: Subscribe to skip adsAdvertisementTechnologyHere’s Why Men’s World Cup Athletes Wear Sports BrasHint: It’s more for science than for supportBGBy Bob Grant5:00 PM CDT on June 23, 2026Share on FacebookShare on X (formerly Twitter)Share on RedditShare on EmailShare on BlueskyIf you’ve been watching as many World Cup games as I have recently, you’ve probably caught a glimpse of a singular undergarment worn by players on the pitches beneath their jerseys: a sports bra. Well, it’s not exactly a sports bra, despite its looks. And it has less to do with corporeal support than it does with tracking biometric data as these world class athletes ply their trade.Subscribe to skip adsFeatured VideoThese harnesses are designed to link up to GPS to track players’ movements around the pitch, both during games and in practice. By providing coaches and trainers with data on heart rate, velocity, acceleration, strain and other metrics. Trends in these data help team managers make informed decisions about when to rest, replace, or push players.But why the tight-fitting, bra-mimicking design? Apparently, the data logger and GPS unit that fits into the upper back of the garment attains the optimal signal when positioned thusly. And to collect biometric data sensors must be worn close to the skin.Already have an account?Log inJoin to continue readingYou've run out of free articles.
